title: Claims Cite
problem: Solva's wedge is "every recommendation is source cited from the claim/policy documents" - i.e., they're betting that citations are the trust primitive insurers buy on. But the public artifact that proves the citations are load bearing under adversarial conditions does not exist. Concretely: when a Solva agent says "this claim violates Section 7.
direction: A reproducible benchmark + verification toolkit that scores any claims handling AI on citation faithfulness under adversarial document perturbations, designed to be the artifact a carrier procurement team can run before signing Solva.
why: Herman scaled Zaver under bank regulation; Sorena ran InsurTech at Citi; Linus shipped regulated AI. Their first instinct on any product decision is "does this stand up to a regulator's audit?" claims cite is the audit artifact made portable. It will land emotionally as well as technically.

archetypes.name: solva evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: solva signal changes while wedge context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: wedge boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: wedge handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: recommendation regression harness
archetypes.trigger: recommendation behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: source operator packet
archetypes.trigger: source output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
